page: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge is an instance within the AWS Inferentia Gen1 family. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a speed of 2.5 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard compute and memory tasks. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks fall within the average range, with scores of 1495.28 and 7874.32 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only speed of 439.45 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 50.99 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In terms of database and web operations, the instance achieves an average of 7.75 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 6.88 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

While the instance maintains average performance in general computing, text processing, and image processing, it exhibits weaker results in specific multi-threaded and language model tasks. The bzip3 multi-threaded decompression is rated as weak, and LLM inference speeds for prompt processing on a medium 7B model are poor at 11.45 tokens/sec. Consequently, this instance is best suited for general-purpose compute workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight database operations rather than heavy multi-threaded decompression or large-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge is an x86_64 instance in the AWS Inferentia Gen1 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. It offers 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average performance across standard benchmarks, including a Passmark CPU score of 8870.98 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.75 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM prompt processing. This hardware profile is best aligned with general-purpose compute tasks, static web serving, and moderate database operations where extreme memory-to-CPU ratios or local storage are not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge is an x86_64 instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor, delivering average performance for general compute and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.2xlarge is an x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: An x86_64 compute instance featuring eight dedicated vCPUs and sixteen gigabytes of DDR4 memory for standard web and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores
16.0 GB of DDR4 system memory running at 3 GHz
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in Redis database operations and static web serving
Weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and LLM prompt processing
Suited for general-purpose compute, web serving, and moderate database tasks
